Garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, GARBAGE.

Some people need to touch the stove for themselves to see that it's hot, and I am one of them. Despite the reviews here and on every printing site, I still had to try one of these. Did I get it to work? A couple of times...but that was only through sheer luck, and I'm still not sure if the joins would hold up to going through the extruder. Of the five "successful" welds I got (out of at least a hundred tries), three of them snapped rolling the filament back on to the line.

Besides the fact that there are no instructions, the design concept itself is flawed. The IDEA is that you get the filament melty, then push the pieces together and then pull through the sizing block. The problem is, unless the filament is completely straight, it's not going to thread from the heating gap into the sizing hole. What kinds of filament will you be trying to join? The last bit on the spool. What is the last bit on the spool always like? Yup, super tightly wound and curved. Even if you straighten that out, what does hot filament do? It droops.

So IF you have someone to help you (two hands to hold the filament pieces, one to hold the lighter and maybe one to hold the welder its
top_profane.title: Utter garbage

I also had a blacklight I'd previously bought to hunt down cat piss so I waited til night time and went to town. I drenched the carpets, baseboards, walls and furniture that my kitty had claimed to be his own. The smell of the solution is very strong so I did have to wear a mask and would take breaks, but it was worth it. top_profane.text:
Is it weird to be in love with a trash can?

For 7 years, I've been hanging on to my old trash can. I never really enjoyed my old trash can (I found it at Walmart and it was the cheapest one available) but I just cringe at the thought of paying $100 for something that holds trash! Well my old trash can was in pretty bad shape. It wasn't really tough to begin with and after 7 years of abuse, the handle had long broken off, the bottom was coming undone, but it still technically "worked". Well a few weeks ago I took the trash out and when I came back home my trash was gone, but so was my trash can. Now I suppose it's possible that someone stole my poor little trash can but I just don't see why when my neighbors have definitely got some better options. My best guess is that my trash guys decided that my trash can needed to go.

Fast forward to the now, tomorrow morning is trash day and I'm a little excited. I took the trash out tonight because I can't bring myself to wake up at 5:00 am when my trash guys get here. top_profane.title: Looooove my trash can!

My profession is custodial, so I’ve been around the Uline 32 gallon trash cans for quite some time. I’ve been in a hospital setting, retail setting, and school setting. Currently trying to work on a project and these are just perfect. They’re not so big, but not so small. I do have to bend over when adding a bag or taking it out, but I’m 5’10. Anyone over 6’0 I recommend investing in a 32 gallon or an industrial trash can with wheels already attached.

The design is simple, much like the Ulines. However, the Ulines have a narrow opening or sometimes a hole you can stick the trash bag through for easy removal and simple, fast application. These lack such a feature, but its not all that special as you can still perfectly fit a trash bag with ease.

The cons about the Ulines is they’re so big that you either have to leave them in one place, or have the wheels attached. This 20 gallon size is perfect for ANYTHING. It’s lightweight enough by itself, and the handles make it super easy to carry over your shoulder. It’s not so big as to when you walk, the bottom hits your hamstring/back of knee; it’s just a great size.

top_profane.title: Some very good trash cans

I'll be totally honest. I've always wanted a simplehuman trash can for my kitchen, but I could never justify paying what I considered to be "big money" for a trash can. So for the last 20 years or so, my kitchen has been the home to cheap plastic Rubbermaid trash cans with a foot pedal to open and close them. Such cans are cheap, which is good. But they really aren't ideal. They get dirty very easily, the bag doesn't always stay in place, and they break. So, when I was given the opportunity to review this beautiful simplehuman trash can after 20 years of using "trashy" trash cans...well, I jumped at the chance.

The trash can arrived at my house packed very carefully: A box within a box, with lots of "air pillows" to take up any extra space. When I unpacked it, I was really surprised at how beautiful this trash can really is. Beautiful AND well-constructed! It's a little taller than the trash cans I'm used to using, but I'm sure I'll adjust to that. The brushed stainless is top-notch and I just love the simplicity of this can. The top lifts completely off to provide easy access to the inner plastic bucket that holds the bag. This makes removing a full bag and replacing it with an empty bag very simple. I also love that you can fold the top of the bag over the inner bucket so that none of the bag shows after you put the top back on. It's a very clean look.

top_profane.text:
Couldn't find a step trash can this large locally, so bought this model online. The shipping dept put the trash can in a box and then the box inside another box for extra care and no damage during shipping. When the huge box arrived I followed the instructions to assemble the trash can and had a trash bag in the thing within a few minutes. The real treat is on the inside where the walls are smooth plastic and nothing snags the trash bag when trying to remove it to take it outside to the garbage bin. Also, the larger size means it can hold 30-32 gallon trash bags, which equates to less trips out to the garbage bin. I chose the black color over tan because food stains and grease spills won't show up as easy on the black plastic vs a lighter tan model.

I wouldn't use this as an outside trash can though, because squirrels and racoons like to sharpen their teeth on the plastic in the fall and you will end up with a bunch of chew marks and holes in your trash can. Only use galvanized metal trash cans if you don't want the night critters chewing a hole in your trash can, tearing up your trash bags, and then scattering food debris all over your back yard.
top_profane.title: Works like a Boss

-Interface-
The interface is terribly unresponsive using the remote. I find I have to press the same button two or three times to get the menu to change. The reason: the component that you point the remote at is a piece of crap. Its a low quality transistor receiver that was poorly assembled, and it was also showing signs of rust. Now rust inside your electronics: first sign of low quality equipment. Technical mumbo-jumbo aside, it is not a very pretty interface compared to other multimedia players I've seen. top_profane.text:
The iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can, made in China, measures 8.9 x 6.4 x 10.3 inches which is smaller than two gallons of milk placed side by side. The trash can with its thick bottom and side which seem to get thinner towards the top weighs 1.8 pounds.

I could use a couple small trash cans for the bathrooms. The iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can seems too small for typical business use, but its compact size seems perfect for squeezing in between a cabinet and toilet. No thought was given to the trash can’s appearance, although the trash can is functional.

I didn’t test the trash can’s fire resistance, however fiberglass is an inorganic fire resistant compound. Fiberglass items that burn tend to be manufactured with burnable epoxies. It shouldn’t be that difficult to create a fire-resistant fiberglass trash can simply by selecting the right ingredients to blend the fiberglass with.

The iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can is compact and fire-resistant. While the durable trash can may be aesthetically displeasing, it will perform its task well. If you’re looking for a small trash can and fire resistance is a concern, the iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can will get the job done.
top_profane.title: Basic: An iLIVING Commercial Fiberglass Fire-Resistant 7 Quart Rectangular Trash Can Review

top_rant.text:
Do I need to say it again? This is a horribly designed printer from a horrible company.

Print quality:
While this is a kit printer, and while most printers have the ability to produce a high quality print, THIS PRINTER CANNOT. You may see a couple people with high-quality prints, but trust me, that is not repeatable without heavy modification to the printer. And I'm talking HEAVY. Modifications. Why? The basalt or MDF print bed is not flat, it droops down towards the center. Your prints will lift off the bed due to poor adhesion on small prints, or there will be heavy Z-Axis banding (looks like waves on the side of the print). You have a choice of a failed print or a crappy print, especially if the print covers a large area. And since this printer does not come with endstops, you're more likely to get a failed print anyways.

Reliability:
This thing is THE FARTHEST THING FROM RELIABLE. I've had this printer for 4 months now. 2 of those months were spend waiting for replacement parts to ship, because this thing has broken down more times than I can count. In fact, the reason why I've finally decided to write a review is because the horrible design of the printer has chewed up one of my more expensive modifications to the printer. The MDF frame is weak, and screws will strip the wood easily. The belts are horribly positioned on the X and Y axis. The
top_rant.title: DO. NOT. BUY. THIS. PRINTER.

It's not exactly the type of packaging that is going to catch your eye at a retail outlet like say the ALO Audio amplifier packaging but I don't particularly care about the card board they wrap products in. I will admit that the ALO Audio boxes that my portable rigs came in serve as cord storage for my desktop system because their Marshall Amp aesthetic is nice enough to keep out on the desktop, but the fact that Firestone didn't put more into their packaging is a far cry from a deal breaker. Inside the box you'll find a warranty card, a quick start users manual, a small wall wart power supply, USB 2.0 cable and a thick though cheap looking RCA cable.

I read the reviews here and found that many of the negative reviews are not accurate, at least not in terms of what I've experienced. In general this is a great machine that works really well with all the dual extrusion and IDEX capabilities. It probably isn't for a beginner but someone that is willing to spend time working thru setup and calibration as this is a critical part of making this machine work correctly.

Unfortunately the documentation isn't necessarily up to date, many of the documents reflect the machine before the upgraded extruders so determining the steps (x, y, z, e) has to be done manually, not a problem but it isn't - build it, boot it and start printing dual extrusions - you'll need to work thru the calibration steps (pretty easy to do).

Pros:
1. Capable IDEX machine
2. Prints multiple materials, multiple colors
3. Excellent support (if used thru Amazon)
4. Well made and easy to put together
5. Nice UI that is pretty intuitive and a responsive touchscreen
6. Extruder 1 to 240c, Extruder 2 to 300c, Bed to 110c
7. Direct extrusion (no Bowden BS)

Cons:
1. Cable management (very minor to me and can be augmented using 3D printed parts)
2. Power supply

top_screaming.text: READ ALL OF THE REVIEWS. YOU ARE NOT BUYING A TOP SHELF PRODUCT. SO, IF YOU DO SMALL PROJECTS, IT IS SUPERB. I USE MINE FOR DIY E JUICE. THE MOST I RUN ON IT IS 180ML AS PG AND ESPECIALLY VG IS VERY THICK. FOR DIY E JUICE, I RECOMMEND THE SAME AS ANOTHER REVIEW, START WITH THE SPINNER (COMES WITH ONE) IN THE BEAKER. ADD 50ML JUICE AND TURN ON. SLOWLY ADD THE REST OF YOUR MIXTURE, AND IT WILL PULL AN 80ML DEEP VORTEX. START OUT BY CENTERING YOUR SPIN BAR, AND THEN SLIDE BEAKER AROUND TO FIND A GOOD DEEP VORTEX. THE SPINBAR THAT COMES WITH IT IS ONE INCH LONG. I USE A ONE AND ONE HALF INCH SPINBAR, SOLD ON AMAZON, AND I FIND IT WORKS BETTER IN THICKER SUBSTRATE. THE LARGER SPINBAR WILL CAUSE THE BEAKER TO HUM A BIT, BUT I PUT A TUPPERWARE BOWL ON THE BEAKER, AND A BARGLASS ON TOP OF THAT. THE HUM WILL CAUSE THE BEAKER TO MOVE AROUND, BUT NOT A LOT. I DO NOT THINK IT WOULD COME OFF THE INSTRUMENTS MAGNETIC FIELD, BUT, I COVER MY E JUICE EVERY TIME, DO NOT WANT TO BREATHE IN DUST. SOME REVIEWS SAID IT HUMS AFTER SHUT OFF. MINE DOES NOT. YOU HAVE TO TURN THE SPEED SWITCH COUNTER CLOCKWISE UNTIL IT CLICKS OFF. THE BOOK SAYS TO UNPLUG IT WHEN DONE FOR THE DAY, I DO, AND HAVE NO NOISE AT ALL. REMEMBER FOLKS, THIS IS NOT A HEAVY DUTY SPINNER. IF YOU NEED A HEAVY DUTY ONE, THEN FORK OUT THE BUCKS AND GET A COUPLE OF HUNDRED DOLLAR ONE ALSO ON AMAZON. SOME REVIEWS TALK ABOUT DURABILITY IS
top_screaming.title: GREAT FOR DIY E JUICE

top_screaming.text: THIS WAS PREVIOUSLY BRANDED AS "MUSE". THE NAMES MEAN NOTHING. IT IS JUST ANOTHER CHINA MADE  RE-BRANDED "CHI-FI"  COMMODITY.  IT FEATURES AN OLDER BUT STILL EXCELLENT SOUNDING TEXAS INSTRUMENTS PCM2704 DECODER CHIP. IT IS ENCLOSED IN AN ALL METAL CASE.  IT HAS A HEADPHONE JACK, BUT NO VOLUME CONTROL. YOU USE YOUR PC TO ADJUST THE VOLUME. I TRIED IT WITH MY BEYERDYNAMIC DT-880 (250 OHM) HEADPHONES AND IT SOUNDS GOOD. IT HAS A RED L.E.D TO INDICATE WHEN IT IS CONNECTED. IT DOES NOT HAVE A POWER SWITCH.  IT DOES NOT USE A PLUG IN POWER SUPPLY. IT IS POWERED BY THE USB PORT ON YOUR P.C.  I'M CURRENTLY  RUNNING IT  TO MY  HEADPHONE AMP AND IT SOUNDS VERY GOOD. MUCH BETTER THAN IT HAS A RIGHT TO FOR ONLY $30.00!!!! .  PLEASE NOTE THAT THE SINGE COAXIAL RCA AND OPTICAL CONNECTION ON THIS DAC ARE OUTPUTS ONLY.  THIS MEANS YOU CAN NOT USE THESE JACKS TO CONNECT YOUR YOUR CD PLAYER FOR USE AS A  TRANSPORT.  BEING AN ENTRY LEVEL USB DAC, YOU SHOULD  TRY THIS  ONE FIRST.  COMPARE IT TO ANYTHING UP TO $200.00  MINE HAS BEEN  WORKING GREAT  24/7.  YOU WILL NEED TO DISABLE THE SOUND CARD SETTING  IN WINDOWS AND ENABLE THE PROPER SETTING.  (IT'S  SIMPLE TO DO). THIS  DAC NEEDS NO SOFTWARE OR PLUG IN POWER SUPPLY TO WORK. THIS CHEAP  DAC IS GREAT DAC FOR LISTENING TO MUSIC VIA PC, WAY BETTER THAN THE BUILT IN SOUND CARD IN ANY COMPUTER, YET IT COSTS NEXT TO NOTHING .  IF OR WHEN YOU WANT TO UP

top_screaming.text: I BOUGHT THESE FOR REPAIRS TO THE DRIP SYSTEM IN MY YARD. THE MAIN LINE IS A 1/2" POLY PIPE WHICH FEEDS THE SMALLER 1/4" PIPES AT THE INDIVIDUAL LOCATIONS. I INTIALLY ORDERED THE #4 SIZE, WHICH WOULD GO AROUND THE PIPE, BUT WHEN A FITTING WAS INSERTED, THE CLAMPS WERE TOO SMALL. I THEN ORDERED THE #8 SIZE, BUT FOUND THEM TOO LARGE. I FINALLY GOT THE CORRECT SIZE IN THE #6 CLAMPS WHICH FIT LIKE THEY SHOULD.THERE SEEMS TO BE A VARIANCE IN WHAT A PARTICULAR # SIZE IS AS I HAVE BOUGHT OTHER BRAND CLAMPS IN THE #4 SIZE WHICH FIT CORRECTLY, BUT NOT THESE. ANYHOW, THE #6 CLAMP FROM THIS MANUFACTURER IS CORRECT FOR DRIP SYSYEMS. IT IS IMPORTANT TO REMEMBER THAT NOT ALL CLAMPS ARE CREATED EQUAL. MOST OF THE CLAMPS SOLD USE STAINLESS STEEL BAND,BUT A COMMON STEEL SCREW. THEY ARE USELESS IN A DRIP SYSTEM EXPOSED TO THE WEATHER AND UNDERGROUND USE. WITHIN A YEAR YOU WILL FIND THE SCREW HAS CORRODED MAKING THE CLAMP UNRELIABLE AND DIFFICULT IF NOT IMPOSSIBLE TO REMOVE. THESE ARE THE CLAMPS YOU WILL FIND PRICED AT 25-35 CENTS APIECE. THESE CLAMPS USE A STAINLESS STEEL SCREW AND BAND WHICH CAN TOLERATE THE WEATHER AND UNDERGROUND USE WITHOUT CORRODING. CLAMPS WITH STAINLESS STEEL SCREWS AS WELL AS BANDS ARE TWO TO THREE TIMES MORE EXPENSIVE THAN THOSE WITH STEEL SCREWS, BUT THESE WERE REASONABLY PRICED AND WELL WORTH PAYING SLIGHTLY MORE FOR THE PEACE OF MIND THAT THEY WILL STILL BE INTACT FI
top_screaming.title: I FINALLY GOT THE CORRECT SIZE IN THE #6 CLAMPS WHICH FIT LIKE THEY SHOULD

top_exclaim.text: [[VIDEOID:5770035f9b47009ed5ec65e5fba96ea2]] I've been trying to remove latex paint from my kitchen cabinets for forever. Paint stripper takes forever, it's a mess to clean up and it's a pain to deal with the gooey paint afterwards. I've got an oscillating tool but the sandpaper packs up with the latex super fast so you're burning through those. I thought I would give these a try and holy crap I am in love. They do remove a decent amount of base material so you have to be careful but these things take the paint off super easy. In my video I'm just removing a little bit of paint with one of these abrasive wheels that I've been using for probably an hour or two. I probably won't need more than 10 of these to finish my entire kitchen, they last forever. I am beyond excited, I have been trying to figure out a way to do this that wouldn't take me 6 months to get everything removed. Granted, these are small so I'm not doing large surfaces but for the edges, rounded corners, and little areas, these things are a gift from God. Now that I know how well they work for what I'm using them for, I would have paid $20 for one wheel. I'm so freaking excited, now I have to go find some that are a lot bigger to do the main areas. IM SO EXCITED!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
top_exclaim.title: OMG THESE ARE THE ABSOLUTE BEST THING EVER!!!!!!!

I luv the color of this filament. The parts have been a hit and miss. After playing with the settings for the longest time with my friend, I noticed what can only think is moisture in the filament. It looks like bubbles when it comes out of the nozzle. Working on drying out and will post the results.

Stuck it in the oven. got it to work again. But looks like it absorbed humidity again. VERY FINICKY filament!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! I need to only work with this filament at home (work apparently is very humid?). The settings I use are 240C nozzle, 80-83C bed, 30mm/s MAX. If it weren't for the BEAUTIFUL green color, I'd throw this filament away. Patience!!

Can't wait to print all of the upgraded parts and forget about this green novamaker filament.

DON'T BUY IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

CONS:
None actually cept If Id known this was a PLA+ Id have ordered it sooner!!!!!

In General:
Now In My Useage The SUNLU worked and printed quite well.
Setup was simple, I use Sticky Blue painters tape.
Set Nozzle at 259 and Bed 109 and let warmup for 10-15 mins.
I set 1st Layer at Nozzle 257 Bed 100, Speed at 35 or 40!
Then after 1st layer is down and hard I set the rest of the run at about say:
Nozzle 250- or 255 Bed 60 0r 75 and let Speed run at 45...
True "I Know" at those settings Ill get some stringing but 'I dont have any problems with layers not bonding and It don't take 'All Day' to get the part finished either.
Yes I know there are all kinds of 'Micro-settings, But I know

top_five_star_obscene.text: This trash can is truly commercial and you can tell the second you go to pick it up! The trash can has some weight to it unlike other plastic trash can you can purchase at the big box local retailers. The lid has a metal mechanism that lifts it, and that metal continues to the the foot that operates the lid. The color online is a little off, it is a little bit brighter red but it is nice. The outside of the trash can is textured so its not smooth but still cleans ups very easily. The can does hold a 13 can bag without having to stretch the crap out of it, and is held in place by to holders that keep it from falling in! Also worth mentioning is the food odor stays in the can, you can clearly tell when open the lid and get say an onion smell.  I like this trash can, probably will get a blue one for my recyclables! Its worth the money!

Used these to put wheels on our kitchen trash can. They roll easily over the vinyl flooring, and carpet. Nice compact size, but heavy duty enough for my purposes.

In case you are curious, I cut a piece of 3/4 inch plywood the same shape and size as the bottom of the trash can to give it some weight, something to screw the casters onto and to attach the trash to. top_five_star_obscene.text: Finally! I have been searching for years to find a reliable garbage can. I consistently buy garbage cans that crack and break and I have to attach the ever-dreaded "GARBAGE" sign with Scotch tape to the thing, hoping the garbage men will see it and haul the piece of decrepit junk away, rather than continuing to throw it back into my yard. Not this time! This garbage can is the real deal (and, funny enough, after buying this one, what do I see at nearly every neighbor on my block's driveway the following garbage day? The exact same garbage can! I wonder if they too got fed up with the cans you find in the local department stores and bought this can from Amazon.) I was disappointed that it was only plastic, but it is incredibly durable plastic that holds up against all kinds of weather and no matter what weight you put into it. The one thing I can never figure out, though, is why they don't put little holes on the bottom to let the rain out. These cans are heavy enough as it is without the addition of rainwater, but I guess that would compromise their strength.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Finally - a good garbage can!

The cleaning staff in our small office replaces about 50 trash bags a day. Even if you just throw a single sticky note in the trash, they take your trash bag and give you a new one. What a waste! This is just the right size for a day's worth of rubbish in my cubicle. For any sticky or wet waste, I walk that to my co-worker's trash can in the next cubicle or the office kitchen. I have matching black mesh pen cup and monitor stands, so it looks like it is part of a set.

top_five_star_obscene.text: These trash cans are very solidly built and hold a lot (55 gallons). My city has large trash bins for regular trash and recycling, but no bin for yard trash. In Spring, Summer, and Fall I have lots of yard trash, and a hard receptacle is one way the county allows us to use for placing at the curb for pickup. These cans are perfect for that purpose. They would also work well for regular trash. Given the large size, they may look large in small kitchens. The lid may have to be ordered separately depending on how the product is listed. These sturdy cans should last a long time with normal usage. While they may be in the sun a lot if used for pickup from the street, they should hold up to the sun or elements well. I recommend these trash cans.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Solidly Built Large Trash Cans

top_five_star_obscene.text:
This little waterproof trash can is made of a smooth, easy-to-clean material so it can be wiped down quickly to stay attractive and modern-looking.

The infrared sensors open and close the little trash can without our having to touch anything, which is nice, particularly when hands are full of whatever we're about to throw out. While it is an attractive can, it is easy to hide and easy to store because it is small and fits into most of my lower cabinet spaces.

The trash can is also airtight so it holds in smells from food or other odorous trash. The garbage bags fit easily into the can and the top of the can is removable for easy emptying and cleaning.

What I like most about this little touchless trash can is that it opens and closes without a sound, and the detachable bucket ring holds the trash bag nicely in place so there is no slippage. Overall, we are quite pleased!

Also worth mentioning that it is rechargeable with the provided USB cable. No batteries to change. Yes!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Touchless trashcan works like a charm!
